steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes that former Spanish colonies still speak Spanish (Cuba example), then shifts to the Netherlands, which also left the Spanish Empire but where Spanish is rarely spoken. The blank must signal this opposite outcome.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'However, the Netherlands was also a former colony': This simply restates that the Netherlands was a colony without signaling the contrast in language outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Similarly, in the Netherlands': 'Similarly' signals sameness, but the Netherlands is the opposite case — Spanish is rarely spoken there. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The reverse is true of the Netherlands': Explicitly signals a flip from the Cuba example to its opposite outcome. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Netherlands, however, is different': Vague — 'different' in what way? It fails to clearly signal the language contrast. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
